mnemonics.title: Heapify Up/Down Memory
mnemonics.content:
SIFT-UP: You SIFT UP when you want to rise to the TOP (like bubbles rising) - used after INSERT.
SIFT-DOWN: You SIFT DOWN when you want to find your PLACE - used after EXTRACT.

mnemonics.title: Heap Property Mnemonic
mnemonics.content:
MAX-heap: MAXimum at TOP - Parent is BIGGER than children.
MIN-heap: MINimum at TOP - Parent is SMALLER than children.
Remember: MAX = BIG on TOP, MIN = SMALL on TOP.

mnemonics.title: Array Index Formulas
mnemonics.content:
PARENT: (i-1)/2 - Subtract 1, then divide by 2
LEFT: 2i+1 - Double i and add 1
RIGHT: 2i+2 - Double i and add 2
For 1-indexed: Just divide or multiply by 2!
PARENT: i/2, LEFT: 2i, RIGHT: 2i+1

acronyms.expansion: Hierarchical Complete Binary Tree with Element Array Property
acronyms.usage: Binary Heap = Complete Binary Tree + Heap Property, stored in array
Queue property - not applicable to heap (heap is NOT FIFO)
Stack property - not applicable to heap

visualTips.visualization: Draw tree level by level, left to right. Number nodes 0,1,2,... Now you have the array! Index 0 is root, index 1-2 are level 1, indices 3-6 are level 2.
Think of a heavy rock (the violating node) sinking down. At each level, it checks children and swaps with the LARGER child (in max-heap). The rock keeps sinking until it floats (no violation).
Think of a bubble rising. The new element is a bubble that floats up by swapping with parent until it's in the right position (no violation).

analogies.analogy: Think of a hospital emergency room. The most critical patient (minimum wait time/max priority) is always seen first - like the root of a min-heap. New patients join the queue (insert), and the doctor always sees the most critical one (extract-min).
Imagine organizing papers by repeatedly taking the most important document and placing it aside, then reorganizing the remaining. That's heap sort - extract max/min repeatedly until all sorted.

analogies.visualization: Think of seating in a cinema. You fill seats row by row, left to right. No empty seat can have someone sitting to its right. That's why array representation works perfectly!
